findings.description: Out-of-scope files in branch diff: merge_pr.py, gate_logic.py, and test_gate_logic.py are modified on this branch but belong to no package scope. They appear to come from the merge of the original PR #67 branch (based on an older main). These should be rebased out or reverted before merge to avoid unintended changes landing on main.
findings.resolution: Rebase the branch onto current main to drop the unrelated file changes, or revert those specific files to their main versions.

findings.description: Wildcard SSRF matching with endswith('.domain.com') could theoretically match 'notadomain.com' if a subdomain suffix collision exists. In practice, the operator controls their own COORDINATION_ALLOWED_HOSTS value, so this is a self-inflicted misconfiguration at worst — not an external attack vector.
findings.resolution: Accept as-is. The operator controls the allowlist. Adding a dot-boundary check (e.g., checking the character before the suffix is a dot or the hostname equals the suffix minus the leading dot) would be more precise but adds complexity for no practical security gain.

findings.description: The .env.example file was not updated with CUSTOM_DOMAIN, TUNNEL_UUID, and CREDENTIALS_FILE variables as specified in task 1.6. The file is in a permission-denied directory from this worktree, but the task explicitly lists it.
findings.resolution: Update agent-coordinator/.env.example with the new environment variables after rebasing, or defer to cleanup phase.

findings.description: Profile cloudflare.yaml uses '*.${CUSTOM_DOMAIN}' which resolves to literal '*.${CUSTOM_DOMAIN}' if CUSTOM_DOMAIN env var is unset (profile loader leaves unresolvable vars as-is). This would add a non-matching wildcard to the SSRF allowlist — harmless but potentially confusing.
findings.resolution: Accept. If CUSTOM_DOMAIN is unset, the wildcard is inert. The profile comments document CUSTOM_DOMAIN as required.

findings.description: Test function test_validate_url_allows_custom_domain tests HTTPS with port 443 explicitly — redundant since 443 is the default HTTPS port. Not harmful but slightly misleading.
findings.resolution: Accept as-is. The test documents that port-qualified URLs work correctly.
